question:
Q.1 Identify the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom:
**A piece of cake**
explanation:
• **An easy task**: Something that is very simple to do. (Related: Child's play, Effortless). 🍰
• **A difficult job**: Something that requires a lot of effort or skill. (Antonym: Up-hill task). 🏔️

question:
Q.2 Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom:
Stop **beating around the bush** and tell me what you want.
explanation:
• **To avoid the main topic**: Discussing a matter without coming to the point. (Related: Prevaricate, Equivocate). 🌳
• **To work hard**: Putting in a lot of physical or mental effort. (Related: Burn the midnight oil). 💡

question: Q.3 What is the meaning of the idiom **'Give a cold shoulder'**?
explanation:
• **To ignore someone intentionally**: To be unfriendly or treat someone with disdain. (Related: Snub, Cut someone dead). ❄️
• **To offer help**: To provide assistance to someone. (Related: Lend a hand). 🤝

question:
Q.4 Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom:
**A blessing in disguise**
options: Something good that isn't recognized at first
answer: Something good that isn't recognized at first
explanation:
• **Something good**: A misfortune that eventually results in something positive. (Related: Silver lining). ✨
• **A hidden problem**: Something that seems good but is actually bad. (Antonym: Snake in the grass). 🐍

question: Q.5 Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: **Bury the hatchet**
explanation:
• **To end a conflict**: To make peace or become friends again after a quarrel. (Related: Make up, Call a truce). 🪓
• **To start a fight**: To begin a quarrel or war. (Antonym: Pick a quarrel). ⚔️

question: Q.6 **Once in a blue moon** means:
explanation:
• **Very rarely**: Something that happens very seldom. (Related: Infrequently, Seldom). 🌙
• **Very frequently**: Happening many times at short intervals. (Antonym: Often, Regularly). 🔁

question: Q.7 If someone is feeling **'under the weather'**, they are:
explanation:
• **Feeling sick**: Feeling slightly ill or not well. (Related: Ailing, Out of sorts). 🤒
• **In trouble**: To be in a difficult or dangerous situation. (Related: In a soup, In hot water). 🥣

question: Q.8 Select the synonym for the idiom: **Spill the beans**
explanation:
• **To reveal a secret**: To disclose confidential information prematurely or accidentally. (Related: Let the cat out of the bag). 🫘
• **To waste time**: To spend time in an unproductive way. (Related: Fritter away). ⏳

question: Q.9 A **'red-letter day'** is:
explanation:
• **An important day**: A day that is pleasantly noteworthy or memorable. (Related: Golden day, Landmark day). 🗓️
• **A dangerous day**: A day filled with threat or risk. (Related: Black day). 🏴

question: Q.10 To support someone **'through thick and thin'** means:
explanation:
• **In all conditions**: Supporting someone through both good and bad times. (Related: Steadfastly, Loyally). 🤝
• **Only in good times**: Supporting someone only when it is convenient. (Related: Fair-weather friend). ☁️

question: Q.11 If you take advice **'with a grain of salt'**, you:
explanation:
• **Don't believe fully**: To view something with skepticism or not take it literally. (Related: Skeptically). 🧂
• **Believe it fully**: To accept something as complete truth. (Antonym). ✅

question: Q.12 A **'wild goose chase'** refers to:
explanation:
• **A useless search**: A foolish and hopeless pursuit of something unattainable. (Related: Futile search). 🦢
• **A successful mission**: A task that achieved its goal. (Antonym). 🎯

question: Q.13 The idiom **'In a nutshell'** means:
explanation:
• **Briefly**: Expressing something in a few words. (Related: Succinctly, To the point). 🌰
• **Extremely difficult**: Something very hard to accomplish. (Related: Herculean task). 💪

question: Q.14 To **'hit the nail on the head'** means:
options: To do or say something exactly right
answer: To do or say something exactly right
explanation:
• **Exactly right**: To describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem. (Related: Precision, Spot on). 🔨
• **To fail a test**: Not succeeding in an examination. (Antonym). ❌

question: Q.15 To **'add fuel to the fire'** means:
options: To make a bad situation worse
answer: To make a bad situation worse
explanation:
• **Make worse**: To aggravate an already difficult or intense situation. (Related: Exacerbate, Fan the flames). 🔥
• **To help someone**: To provide support or assistance. (Antonym). 🤝
